scope.claim_limit: Public-sequence mechanism check on hilly KITTI seq08; not a substitute for dedicated off-road / multi-beam non-flat benchmarks such as MulRan.

ground_on.note: M-GCLO: multiple-ground point-to-plane constraints (vertical accuracy) + non-ground point-to-distribution (NDT) with per-point range-uncertainty weighting; constant-velocity prior, no GT seed. mean_ground/nonground_corr=1072/2205

ground_off.note: M-GCLO: ground factor disabled (non-ground NDT only; classified ground held out) + non-ground point-to-distribution (NDT) with per-point range-uncertainty weighting; constant-velocity prior, no GT seed. mean_ground/nonground_corr=941/2337

conclusion: On public KITTI Odometry seq08 full, disabling the classified ground factor leaves translational RPE nearly unchanged (1.354% vs 1.353%), but whole-run ATE worsens by about 148.6% (21.15 m vs 52.56 m) and rotational RPE rises by about 38.9%. This matches the seq00/07 KITTI ablation pattern: multiple-ground constraints anchor global/attitude stability on hilly public driving data, even when local translational drift stays near KISS-ICP. Dedicated off-road / multi-beam non-flat benchmarks such as MulRan remain open before T0 promotion.
